title: Matroids and the Integral Hodge Conjecture for Abelian Varieties
speaker: Philip Engel
abstract: We will discuss a proof that the integral Hodge conjecture is false for a very general abelian variety of dimension ≥ 4. Associated to any regular matroid is a degeneration of principally polarized abelian varieties. We introduce a new combinatorial invariant of regular matroids, which obstructs the algebraicity of the minimal curve class, on the very general fiber of the associated degeneration. In concert with a result of Voisin, one deduces (via the intermediate Jacobian) the stable irrationality of a very general cubic threefold. This is joint work with Olivier de Gaay Fortman and Stefan Schreieder.
tags: Workshop on Recent Developments in Hodge Theory and O-minimality

proof_stages.label: Regular matroid input
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes the specialized notion of a regular matroid as a vector configuration in Z^g with saturated spans, and introduces cographic and graphic matroids as the key combinatorial sources.

proof_stages.label: Matroidal degeneration of principally polarized abelian varieties
proof_stages.summary: From a regular matroid the speaker constructs a family of principally polarized abelian g-folds over a punctured polydisk by an explicit period map, records its rank-one monodromy, and explains via Mumford’s construction and a hypertorus arrangement why it admits a flat proper nodal filling with smooth total space.

proof_stages.label: Combinatorial divisibility invariant
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem asserts the existence of a combinatorial integer d(r) dividing every algebraic multiple of the minimal curve class on a very general fiber, with d(r)=1 exactly for cographic matroids, and later identifies d(r) through mod-p obstructions.

proof_stages.label: Spreading and moving an algebraic curve to the central fiber
proof_stages.summary: For the proof, a curve representing m times the minimal class is assumed to live over the generic base, spread out, and then—using smoothness of the total space and a moving/Bertini argument—replaced modulo primes p not dividing m by a curve transverse to the central fiber.

proof_stages.label: Colored dual graph and monodromy linear algebra
proof_stages.summary: The limiting nodal curve yields a dual graph whose weight-zero homology maps to the lattice M of the abelian degeneration, whose node colors are the matroid elements, and whose Picard-Lefschetz monodromy gives a mod-p splitting together with quadratic forms satisfying specified compatibility and orthogonality conditions.

proof_stages.label: Universal mod-p Albanese graph obstruction
proof_stages.summary: A purely combinatorial devissage produces a universal r-colored mod-p Albanese graph; the nonexistence of solutions to the extracted linear-algebra conditions for a prime p contradicts p not dividing m, so the product of such primes is the invariant d(r).

proof_stages.label: Applications to abelian varieties and cubic threefolds
proof_stages.summary: Applying the theorem to the graphic matroid of K5 gives failure of the integral Hodge conjecture for very general principally polarized abelian varieties in dimension at least four, while applying it to the R10/Seymour-Bixby matroid arising from the Segre cubic degeneration and using Voisin’s criterion gives stable irrationality of very general cubic threefolds.

examples.name: Rank-two theta matroid
examples.note: The vector configuration {(1,0),(0,1),(1,1)} and its theta-graph realization make concrete the regular-matroid definition, the period-map formula, the hypertorus central fiber, and the later coloring of nodes.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker computes with the rank-two theta matroid vectors, identifies them as a cographic matroid, writes the period matrix, draws the hypertorus arrangement, and analyzes the central fiber and nodal smoothings.
